Clockwork Watchman Bracer  
 


Slot: WRIST
Charges: 10
AC: 10
This item can be used in tradeskills.
Effect: Produce Wrench (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: 5.0) at Level 20
WT: 3.0 Size: SMALL
Class: WAR CLR PAL SHD
Race: GNM
Clockwork Watchman Bracer

Slot: WRIST
Charges: 10
AC: 10
This item can be used in tradeskills.
Effect: Produce Wrench (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: 5.0) at Level 20
WT: 3.0 Size: SMALL
Class: WAR CLR PAL SHD
Race: GNM

Item Type:Armor
Appearance:Velious Plate 2
Tint:
 
Color (RGB):0, 0, 0
Stackable:No
Merchant Value:90 pp 0 gp 0 sp 0 cp
Tribute:730
Lucy Entry By:Kerasota
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-07-27 09:32:51
Page Updated:Tue Mar 13th, 2018

Expansion: Scars of Velious
Average Price: No Data Pricing Data...
Rarity: Common
Level to Attain: 1
[Recipes | Comments ]

Crafted: This item is crafted by players.

    • Class: ALL
    Expansion: Not Set
    Yield: 1 x Clockwork Watchman Bracer

Tinkered

Would not combine with a Fine plate bracer. Toolbox says it requires a small fine plate bracer.
▲ Page top
It can be recharged
QuoteReply
# Jun 06 2001 at 8:39 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591756382893
7 posts
Watchman armor can be recharged.
▲ Page top
RE: It can be recharged
QuoteReply
# Jun 06 2001 at 9:15 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I believe that all you have to do is replace the class battery which is purchased in iceclad at the igloos. All watchman items i believe are rechargeable by doing this.

Gnorm Blackfoot / Cleric / Tinkerer / all around handyman
▲ Page top
RE: It can be recharged
QuoteReply
# Jun 06 2001 at 9:03 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
how do you recharge it i have watchman boots and have zero charges left
▲ Page top
RE: It can be recharged
QuoteReply
# Oct 10 2001 at 8:56 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592249475779
Scholar
148 posts
Place it, along with the appropriate manna battery into a toolbox, press combine. Takes ZERO skill, trivial for even non-gnomes to do.
▲ Page top
I say make it produce wenches!
QuoteReply
# Jun 06 2001 at 7:22 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592248767058

18 posts
I like this guys Idea Bring on da' babes )
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 06 2001 at 6:38 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) and only 10 charges - what a piece of crap
you got it all wrong
QuoteReply
# Jun 05 2001 at 10:01 PM Rating: Decent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
the effect doesn't "Produce" a "Wrench", it makes a "Produce Wrench". Its what all the smart grocery stores use when their Produce is in need of repairs. I mean, you ever seen the bill for a Tomato Repairman? its horrendous!
▲ Page top
RE: you got it all wrong
QuoteReply
# Jun 13 2001 at 3:44 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
<humour>
You know for a moment then I thought "Wow - produce wench, cute gnome girly for me; yeehaa"
</humour>

-ooc I have to use <humour> & </humour> because you Americans don't seem to understand without it.

ermm... I think he used "humour" cause he's a git.
Union Jack loves throwing "U's" into everything. Or maybe it's us damn yanks that take em out? They did invent the English language after all.

Long live the Queen, people. Long live the Queen.

# Jun 05 2001 at 7:30 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592246043090
13 posts
According to The Tinkering Compendium (www.eqportal.com/tinkering), the wrench is a "6dmg 26dly LORE NORENT hammer" summoned item. Not really good for much at the level of a typical Watchman's Armour wearer. *sigh*
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 05 2001 at 6:56 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Ok, obvious question: What does the wrench do once "produced"?
